title: Quantum Histories and Quantum Complementarity
abstract:
We discuss the origin of quantum complementarity using
Feynman's sum-over-histories approach to Quantum Mechanics (QM).
We analyze two experimental setups introduced by W. G. Unruh and
S. Afshar and show how one should consistently calculate the existence
of which-way information using interfering quantum histories. Then we
discuss the possible problems associated with the notion of which way
information and provide an explanation why Englert-Greenberger duality
relation cannot be violated experimentally if the standard QM postulates
are accepted.
